title: Accept subscription-policy gray-zone risk for the interactive worker fleet
context: claude -p moves to API-rate billing. The v2 fleet avoids -p entirely by running hand-started interactive sessions, but fleet-scale automated use of subscription sessions sits in a policy gray zone: mechanically identical to a human running several terminals, yet adjacent to the behavior the billing change targets.

options.name: api-billing-for-workers
options.rationale: Unambiguously compliant; workers run via SDK/API.
options.tradeoff: Per-token costs defeat the project's economic premise (subscription credits).

options.name: accept-gray-zone-with-fallback
options.rationale: Run the interactive fleet on subscription; document the exposure and a concrete fallback.
options.tradeoff: Policy tightening could force a mid-project economics change.

reasoning: Sessions are interactive, hand-started, and human-supervised at gates — defensible as legitimate use. Exposure is bounded and the fallback is concrete: reduce concurrent sessions first; consciously route overflow through API billing if policy tightens. Revisit when the billing change ships.
